item.title: You Digg?
item.description: Digg was just opened to the public in January 2026. After facing a swarm of AI bots, they decided to shutdown once more. I will mourn them in peace again.

item.title: computer graphics, maths, shaders, fractals, demoscene
item.description: Tutorials and articles of Inigo Quilez on computer graphics, fractals, math, art, demoscene, shaders and more.

item.title: GitHub - TomBadash/MouseControl: A lightweight, open-source alternative to Logitech Options+ for remapping buttons on the Logitech MX Master 3S mouse
item.description: A lightweight, open-source alternative to Logitech Options+ for remapping buttons on the Logitech MX Master 3S mouse - TomBadash/MouseControl

item.title: GitHub - Hammerspoon/hammerspoon: Staggeringly powerful macOS desktop automation with Lua

item.title: 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 | Claude
item.description: Standard pricing now applies across the full 1M window for both models, with no long-context premium. Media limits expand to 600 images or PDF pages.

item.title: Your Phone is an Entire Computer
item.description: The new MacBook Neo announcement raises old questions around what rights we have (and don't have) to install software on devices we own.

item.title: How to Run a Pool of Autonomous Coding Agents on Your Jira Backlog
item.description: What if you could point a fleet of AI agents at your Jira backlog and let them work through it continuously?

item.title: Big tech engineers need big egos
item.description: It’s a common position among software engineers that big egos have no place in tech1. This is understandable - we’ve all worked with some insufferably overconfident engineers who needed their egos checked - but I don’t think it’s correct. In fact, I don’t know if it’s possible to survive as a software engineer in a large tech company without some kind of big ego.

item.title: The Model is the Company
item.description: Picture two founders starting a company today.

item.title: Why Your Cache Might Be Slowing You Down
item.description: Thoughts on engineering, distributed systems, system design, and resilience patterns.

item.title: Evaluating Claude’s dbt Skills: Building an Eval from Scratch
Don't Vibe — Prove | NGrislain
item.description: AI can write code fast. But can it write correct code?

item.title: let the code do the talking

item.title: Agentic Abuse at Tech Jobs
item.description: The classic anti-patterns at tech jobs are back, newly evolved for the early agentic AI era. As the machines get smarter, the humans are doubling down on bad strategies that never worked before and wo
item.author: (https://blog.alejandrowainzinger.com/@xevix)

item.title: README, Don't AGENTS.md Me
item.description:
This is the place where I rant that the AGENTS.md pattern is a distraction and slows down your software development.
The software engineering industry has had a standard of adding a README.md file to the root of projects and also the root of an...
item.author: (https://www.joshbeckman.org/about/)

item.title: Undefined Roles | AndiSmith.com
item.description: With the acceleration of AI, the traditional boundaries between product, engineering and UX are collapsing.

item.title: How Spotify Silently Updates Itself (And How to Stop It)
item.description: Spotify updated itself without my permission. I blocked it. It updated again. I blocked it again. It found a third way. This is the story of reverse-engineer...

item.title: Our Experience with i-Ready
item.description: When you chose a school, you probably didn’t realize you were also choosing educational software.

item.title: Scale is absolutely Broken
item.description: Explore the terrifying symmetry of scale—from the "not-space" beyond the universe to the infinite canyons inside an atom. Learn how axioms and fortune don't just add; they compound at an exponential rate, building a momentum that eventually moves on its own. It’s the mightiest force in the universe, and it’s the only intuition that matters.
